_comment: Hex-grid calibration mapping CCRR codes to FULL-image pixel centers for materials/map/Americas_all.jpg (11772x7566). Flat-top hexes, even-q offset (EVEN columns shifted DOWN by half a row). Column-first CCRR ('0803' = col 08, row 03). Pinned 2026-06-30 interactively with Ray: 3 operator-confirmed anchor hexes (Olympia 0803, Augusta 8405, Puerto Rico 8243) read off the printed map + FFT-measured pitch, least-squares fit (all anchors <1px residual; geometry ratio 1.1540 vs ideal flat-top 1.1547). Independently validated at two UN-FITTED hexes (4025 Oklahoma, 7522 Richmond) where predicted CCRR == printed CCRR. SINGLE-segment uniform model (no seam, unlike TWU's board). ONLY LAND hexes are numbered/playable; ocean is blank (no hexes). Hex labels appear printed at/near the geometric center (NOT offset above like TWU's board) - confirm during terrain sampling.
offset_scheme: even-q (even columns shifted DOWN by half row pitch)

ref_hexes.note: San Juan / Puerto Rico (SE) - operator-confirmed anchor
Oklahoma/Kansas - independent spot-check, printed CCRR matched prediction
Richmond, VA - independent spot-check, printed CCRR matched prediction

origin.note: lattice origin (col 1 row 1 center, ODD column = no even-offset). Cols 1-6 are off-coast Pacific ocean (blank/unnumbered) but the coordinate system extends there; first numbered land column is ~07 at the coast.

x_model.note: x = 204.29 + col*132.21  (col=1 -> 336.5). Single segment, no seam. cpx from Olympia(col8)->Augusta(col84) long baseline + FFT, residual <1px across all anchors.

y_model.note: y = 121.82 + row*152.58 + (col even ? 76.29 : 0)  (row=1 odd-col -> 274.4). Uniform across whole board; least-squares over 3 anchors, residual <1px. Ratio rpy/cpx=1.1540 (ideal flat-top 1.1547).

formula: col=int(CCRR[:2]); row=int(CCRR[2:]); x = 204.29 + col*132.21; y = 121.82 + row*152.58 + ((col%2==0)? 76.29 : 0.0)   [pixels in FULL-image space]
notes: Pinned + independently validated 2026-06-30 (see _comment). Grid spans ~cols 1-89 x rows 1-49; numbered/PLAYABLE hexes are the LAND subset only (ocean is blank, no hexes - mask to land). Puerto Rico (8144/8243 + numbered neighbors) is a DISCONNECTED island cluster on the SAME global lattice, separated from the mainland by blank ocean - not an inset. NEXT (terrain digitization): hex-fill layer (water/clear/forest/towns, calibrated to known-terrain exemplar hexes, nearest-match) + hexside EDGE layer (rivers=thin solid lines, rail=lines, MOUNTAINS=thick brown granulated bands along hexsides). NOTE: mountains are HEXSIDE terrain in GotA, NOT hex-fill - that is why the first-pass interior-sampling classifier found only 2.
